title: Verify catalog through real Medusa Store runtime

gates.name: real Medusa storefront e2e
gates.command: npm --workspace apps/storefront run test:e2e -- catalog product-detail

verify: Storefront sends x-publishable-api-key for Store API requests.
Playwright starts the real Medusa backend, not catalog-e2e-server.cjs.
Browser evidence covers catalog, filters, product detail, variant availability, and Medusa variant-ID handoff.

verify.summary: Windows-native smoke, compiled Medusa Store E2E, publishable-key boundary, catalog/product-detail buyer flows, Medusa variant-ID handoff, unit tests, typecheck, artifact checks, port cleanup, Memory Bank lint, and strict doctor passed.

evidence_required: Playwright output naming the real Medusa backend runtime.
Trace and screenshots for catalog and product detail flows.
HTTP evidence for publishable-key Store API access.
constraints: Use a local publishable API key scoped to the seeded storefront sales channel.
Use the actual Medusa Store API runtime and seeded local PostgreSQL data.
Keep test startup and cleanup deterministic on Windows.
invariants: Do not bypass Store middleware with a test-only backend server.
Do not use production secrets or production data.
Storefront must not query PostgreSQL directly.
purpose: Replace false-success browser evidence with end-to-end proof through the real Medusa Store runtime.
success_outcome: The Next.js storefront browses and selects canonical Medusa products and variants through publishable-key-scoped Store APIs.
anti_goals: Do not add production deployment behavior.
Do not create a second backend harness.
Do not implement cart persistence beyond the existing narrow handoff.

stop_conditions: A local publishable key cannot be created and scoped deterministically.
Real Medusa runtime cannot expose the required catalog contracts.
E2E requires production or live-provider access.
